results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE PURPOSE OF THE PROJECT IS TO CREATE A COMMUNICATION INFRASTRUCTURE IN COLORADO FOR AIR QUALITY INFORMATION THAT (A) ENHANCES THE AVAILABLE INFORMATION ABOUT AIR QUALITY, (B) DEVELOPS TARGETED MESSAGING THAT REACHES INDIVIDUALS BASED ON THEIR LEVELS OF NEED AND VULNERABILITY, AND (C) UTILIZES TRUSTED SOURCES OF INFORMATION. ACTIVITIES:THE PROJECT WILL 1) EXPAND LOW-COST MONITORING NETWORKS IN THE PROJECT'S PARTNER TEST-BED COMMUNITIES IN COLORADO; 2) DEVELOP REAL-TIME SUB-CITY-SCALE SMOKE MAPS TO SUPPORT ACCURATE AND ACTIONABLE COMMUNICATION ABOUT AIR QUALITY; 3) CONDUCT INTERVIEWS WITH COMMUNITY LEADERS AND MEMBERS TO DEVELOP MESSAGING THROUGH MULTIPLE COMMUNICATION CHANNELS IN ORDER TO ENHANCE THE CAPABILITY OF COMMUNITIES TO PROVIDE TIMELY, ACCURATE, AND ACTIONABLE INFORMATION ABOUT AIR QUALITY; AND 4) EVALUATE THE EFFECTIVENESS OF COMMUNICATION CAMPAIGNS IN DEVELOPING INDIVIDUAL-LEVEL AWARENESS, COGNITIVE, AND BEHAVIORAL OUTCOMES.SUBRECIPIENT:OUTCOMES: THE PROJECT WILL PRODUCE REAL-TIME, HIGH-RESOLUTION PARTICULATE MATTER AND OZONE CONCENTRATION MAPS COVERING THE REGIONS OF THE PROJECT'S PARTNER ORGANIZATIONS; DEVELOP BEST PRACTICES FOR COMMUNICATING AIR QUALITY INFORMATION; AND PROVIDE RESEARCH FINDINGS ON EFFECTIVENESS OF THE COMMUNICATION STRATEGIES DEVELOPED BY THE PROJECT. EXPECTED OUTCOMES OF THE PROJECT INCLUDE EXPANDED LOW-COST MONITORING NETWORKS, ENHANCED COMMUNICATION INFRASTRUCTURE IN COLORADO FOR AIR QUALITY INFORMATION, AND IMPROVED UNDERSTANDING OF HOW BEST TO COMMUNICATE HEALTH RISKS AND AIR QUALITY INFORMATION TO ENABLE PROTECTIVE ACTIONS TO REDUCE WILDFIRE SMOKE EXPOSURES. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES OF THIS RESEARCH INCLUDE THE SCIENTIFIC COMMUNITY AND COLORADO RESIDENTS WHO ARE AFFECTED BY WILDFIRE SMOKE.
